verses.1: For the music director; on stringed instruments. A Maskil of David. Listen to my prayer, God; And do not hide Yourself from my pleading.
verses.2: Give Your attention to me and answer me; I am restless in my complaint and severely distracted,
verses.3: Because of the voice of the enemy, Because of the pressure of the wicked; For they bring down trouble upon me And in anger they hold a grudge against me.
verses.4: My heart is in anguish within me, And the terrors of death have fallen upon me.
verses.5: Fear and trembling come upon me, And horror has overwhelmed me.
verses.6: I said, “Oh, that I had wings like a dove! I would fly away and be at rest.
verses.7: “Behold, I would flee far away, I would spend my nights in the wilderness. Selah
verses.8: “I would hurry to my place of refuge From the stormy wind and heavy gale.”
verses.9: Confuse them, Lord, divide their tongues, For I have seen violence and strife in the city.
verses.10: Day and night they go around her upon her walls, And evil and harm are in her midst.
verses.11: Destruction is in her midst; Oppression and deceit do not depart from her streets.
verses.12: For it is not an enemy who taunts me, Then I could endure it; Nor is it one who hates me who has exalted himself against me, Then I could hide myself from him.
verses.13: But it is you, a man my equal, My companion and my confidant;
verses.14: We who had sweet fellowship together, Walked in the house of God among the commotion.
verses.15: May death come deceitfully upon them; May they go down alive to Sheol, For evil is in their dwelling, in their midst.
verses.16: As for me, I shall call upon God, And the LORD will save me.
verses.17: Evening and morning and at noon, I will complain and moan, And He will hear my voice.
verses.18: He will redeem my soul in peace from the battle which is against me, For they are many who are aggressive toward me.
verses.19: God will hear and humiliate them— Even the one who sits enthroned from ancient times— Selah With whom there is no change, And who do not fear God.
verses.20: He has put forth his hands against those who were at peace with him; He has violated his covenant.
verses.21: His speech was smoother than butter, But his heart was war; His words were softer than oil, Yet they were drawn swords.
verses.22: Cast your burden upon the LORD and He will sustain you; He will never allow the righteous to be shaken.
verses.23: But You, God, will bring them down to the pit of destruction; Men of bloodshed and deceit will not live out half their days. But I will trust in You.
